name: bgpt-paper-search
tagline: MCP skill wiring Claude to BGPT's remote paper-search server, returning structured experimental data instead of just abstracts.
verdict: Clean, transparent skill: no shell execution, no credential reads, no hidden instructions, read-only search behavior matching its stated purpose. Minor risks: it depends on a single-maintainer npm package (bgpt-mcp) and a paid third-party server (bgpt.pro) that receives your search queries and, for paid use, an API key — verify the maintainer before enabling

highlights.summary: Setup relies on `npx bgpt-mcp` / `npx mcp-remote` pulling from npm and a proprietary remote server (bgpt.pro) run by a single listed GitHub maintainer (connerlambden/bgpt-mcp) — an unaudited, business
No 'do not use when' guidance is given (e.g., non-scientific search, when abstracts suffice) — a minor omission, not a safety issue.
No guidance on failure paths — e.g. what to do if the free 50-search/network limit is hit, the server is down, or no API key is configured.
